latest.title: Form 8.3 - Aferian PLC
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4394U
Aferian PLC
26 February 2026
FORM 8.3
PUBLIC OPENING POSITION DISCLOSURE/DEALING DISCLOSURE BY
A PERSON WITH INTERESTS IN RELEVANT SECURITIES REPRESENTING 1% OR MORE
Rule 8.3 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")
1. KEY INFORMATION
(a) Full name of discloser:
Hans Disch
(b) Owner or controller of interests and short positions disclosed, if different from 1(a):
The naming of nominee or vehicle companies is insufficient. For a trust, the trustee(s), settlor and beneficiaries must be named.
(c) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:
Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree
Aferian plc
(d) If an exempt fund manager connected with an offeror/offeree, state this and specify identity of offeror/offeree:
(e) Date position held/dealing undertaken:
For an opening position disclosure, state the latest practicable date prior to the disclosure
February 25th, 2026.
(f) In addition to the company in 1(c) above, is the discloser making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?
If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"
n/a
2. POSITIONS OF THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE
If there are positions or rights to subscribe to disclose in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 2(a) or (b) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security.
(a) Interests and short positions in the relevant securities of the offeror or offeree to which the disclosure relates following the dealing (if any)
Class of relevant security:
1p Ordinary
Interests
Short positions
Number
%
Number
%
(1) Relevant securities owned and/or controlled:
7,233,540
6.50%
(2) Cash-settled derivatives:
(3) Stock-settled derivatives (including options) and agreements to purchase/sell:
TOTAL:
7,233,540
6.50%
All interests and all short positions should be disclosed.
Details of any open stock-settled derivative positions (including traded options), or agreements to purchase or sell relevant securities, should be given on a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions).
(b) Rights to subscribe for new securities (including directors' and other employee options)
Class of relevant security in relation to which subscription right exists:
n/a
Details, including nature of the rights concerned and relevant percentages:
n/a
3. DEALINGS (IF ANY) BY THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE
Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 3(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.
The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.
(a) Purchases and sales
Class of relevant security
Purchase/sale
Number of securities
Price per unit
1p Ordinary Shares
Purchase
1,000,000
GBP 0.008
(b) Cash-settled derivative transactions
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. CFD
Nature of dealing
e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position
Number of reference securities
Price per unit
n/a
(c) Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)
(i) Writing, selling, purchasing or varying
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.
Number of securities to which option relates
Exercise price per unit
Type
e.g. American, European etc.
Expiry date
Option money paid/ received per unit
n/a
(ii) Exercise
Class of relevant security
Product description
e.g. call option
Exercising/ exercised against
Number of securities
Exercise price per unit
n/a
(d) Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)
Class of relevant security
Nature of dealing
e.g. subscription, conversion
Details
Price per unit (if applicable)
4. OTHER INFORMATION
(a) Indemnity and other dealing arrangements
Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the person making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:
Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included. If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(b) Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives
Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the person making the disclosure and any other person relating to:
(i) the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or
(ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:
If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"
None
(c) Attachments
Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?
no
Date of disclosure:
February 25th, 2026
Contact name:
Hans Disch
Telephone number*:
+31651216209
Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.
The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.
*If the discloser is a natural person, a telephone number does not need to be included, provided contact information has been provided to the Panel's Market Surveillance Unit.
The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at
www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. company_30s.what_they_do: Aferian PLC is a small‑cap investment holding that focuses on acquiring niche businesses in the UK and providing them with financial and strategic support.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a growing market where many small businesses need capital and expertise to scale. By bringing together a portfolio of such firms, Aferian can create synergies and unlock value that individual players cannot achieve alone.
company_30s.current_state: Aferian is still in its early, cash‑burn phase. Recent filings show that it has secured an extension of its senior banking facility, indicating that it can keep operating for the near term. The business has not yet produced revenue, so the stock is essentially a bet on future growth rather than current earnings.

rns_narrative: The company has extended its senior banking facilities, giving it more runway to pursue acquisitions and operational improvements.
Management has released a trading update that outlines ongoing efforts to raise capital and identify new business opportunities.
There is a growing sense that the company may be in talks with potential acquirers, as indicated by several large buy orders in the last week.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but watchful: small buys hint at hidden interest
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the recent funding extension and the possibility of an acquisition, suggesting that insiders see a path to value.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of revenue, high debt exposure and the fact that the company is still pre‑revenue, flagging a potential liquidity crisis.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community moved from neutral to a mild bullish tone as buy activity increased, but overall sentiment remains subdued.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, which means the market is still unaware of any upcoming catalyst – a perfect setup for a surprise bounce.

near_term_catalysts: Official confirmation that the senior banking facility has been renewed or that a new funding round has closed.
Release of the first revenue figures or a clear go‑to‑market plan for the portfolio companies.
Any public statement that a strategic buyer or investor is interested in taking a stake or acquiring the company.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume, a price break above the 5‑day moving average, or a news headline that mentions a funding or acquisition deal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the late stages of a deep capitulation cycle. The APEX score indicates a tight compression – a classic sign that a mean‑reversion bounce could be imminent if support holds.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has crashed more than 70% from its all‑time high, a situation that historically delivers large rebounds. If the current price is £0.01 and the last peak was £0.10, the halfway point is £0.055 – a 45% upside from today. Because the price is already at the bottom of a deep trough, the potential reward far outweighs the limited downside (you can’t lose more than the current price).
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without any revenue and with a heavy debt load, the company could face a liquidity crunch. If the bank facility is withdrawn or a potential buyer walks away, the share price could collapse to zero. The worst‑case risk is the full loss of the invested capital.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Upside: ~45% if the price recovers to the halfway mark. Downside: 100% of the current price. Risk‑reward ratio roughly 45:1 – a classic asymmetric trade.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once volume spikes and the price clears the 5‑day moving average. Allocate 5–10% of your discretionary equity per position, set a stop 30% below entry, and trail the stop as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a small hedge until you see either a funding confirmation, a revenue announcement, or a clear acquisition interest. Trigger a buy only if one of those catalysts appears.
playbook.if_passing: No immediate terminal risk has surfaced, so the default stance is bullish on a deep‑crash setup that has recently attracted institutional buying.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Aferian’s deep crash, coupled with fresh funding and rising buy activity, sets the stage for a potentially large mean‑reversion bounce – an asymmetric play with high upside and limited downside.
